title: tocoyena foetida
intro: Tocoyena foetida, also known as the stinking logwood or stinking tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Tocoyena foetida include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Tocoyena foetida include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues.
